title: Hypertension follow-up

red_flags.text: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth hypertension scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth hypertension scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Hypertension – 27

red_flags.text: Lists severe blood pressure, confusion, visual changes, chest pain, breathlessness, neurological symptoms, severe headache, and specialist referral criteria.
red_flags.rationale: Supports mapped assessed chest, breathlessness, visual, and focal-neurological negatives.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists severe blood pressure, confusion, visual changes, chest pain, breathlessness, neurological symptoms, severe headache, and specialist referral criteria.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Hypertension – 27

examination.text: Specifies office, repeated home, and ambulatory blood-pressure assessment and hypertension staging.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped hypertension terms, blood-pressure review, blood-pressure examination, preset, and home-log review.
examination.evidence_extract: Specifies office, repeated home, and ambulatory blood-pressure assessment and hypertension staging.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Hypertension – 27

investigations.text: Describes monitoring phases, clinician-judged laboratory review, lifestyle domains, and monthly review context.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped home readings, adherence, lifestyle, history, and documentation-only plan or follow-up fields.
investigations.evidence_extract: Describes monitoring phases, clinician-judged laboratory review, lifestyle domains, and monthly review context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Hypertension – 27

investigations.text: Lists follow-up electrolytes, renal function, lipids, urine ACR, glucose or HbA1c, liver tests, blood count, and ECG according to medication and result context.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped renal, electrolyte, lipid, urine ACR, and ECG review.
investigations.evidence_extract: Lists follow-up electrolytes, renal function, lipids, urine ACR, glucose or HbA1c, liver tests, blood count, and ECG according to medication and result context.

source_limitations: Broad examination prompts beyond blood pressure and generic clinician-entered plans remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, target setting, medicine choice, dose, and emergency classification remain clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
